channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [ANSALAPI] SC upholds NCLAT order on Ansal Properties' CIRP; Promoters to cooperate
item.description:
🔥 Trending

The Supreme Court of India, in its order dated April 16, 2026, disposed of appeals concerning Ansal Properties & Infrastructure Ltd's Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP), affirming the NCLAT's prior decision. The Court chose not to intervene with the NCLAT's order but left certain legal questions open. The company's erstwhile promoters have pledged full cooperation in the ongoing CIRP proceedings, which are directed to advance swiftly. A writ petition filed by homebuyers was also dismissed, with homebuyers advised to pursue other suitable legal avenues. The CIRP is specifically focused on projects in Lucknow and Rajasthan, and the resolution plan for the Serene Residency project was previously approved by NCLT in October 2025.

  • Supreme Court upheld NCLAT order regarding the company's CIRP.
  • Erstwhile promoters committed to full cooperation in CIRP proceedings.
  • CIRP for Lucknow and Rajasthan projects to proceed expeditiously.
  • Homebuyers' writ petition disposed; can pursue other legal remedies.
  • Resolution plan for Serene Residency project was already approved by NCLT.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ANSAL PROPERTIES & INFRASTRUCTURE LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [MAFATIND] Mafatlal Industries Board to meet May 5 for FY26 Results & Dividend
item.description:
Mafatlal Industries Ltd has announced that its Board of Directors will convene on May 5, 2026. The primary agenda includes considering and approving the Annual Audited Standalone and Consolidated Financial Results for the financial year ended March 31, 2026. Additionally, the Board will consider and recommend a final dividend, if any, for the same financial year, subject to shareholder approval at the 112th Annual General Meeting. Other matters include approving the Director's Report and fixing the date for the AGM. The trading window for the company's securities remains closed from April 1, 2026, until 48 hours after the results are made public.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 5, 2026.
  • To approve FY26 Audited Standalone and Consolidated Financial Results.
  • To consider and recommend final dividend for FY26.
  • Trading window closed from April 1, 2026, until results are out.
  • Also to fix date for 112th Annual General Meeting.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: MAFATLAL INDUSTRIES LTD.-$ | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [HARDWYN] Hardwyn Launches ISI-Certified Floor Spring, Secures Institutional Orders
item.description:
Hardwyn India Ltd. has launched its new ISI-certified Floor Spring, designed for reliable glass door control in high-traffic commercial and institutional environments. This product meets stringent Indian safety standards and is available in Silver and Black finishes. Concurrently, the company has secured multiple purchase orders for the new hardware from prestigious institutional and government-backed infrastructure projects across India, including medical colleges and universities in Uttar Pradesh and Chhattisgarh. Management highlighted this as a significant step in strengthening their product portfolio and reinforcing market trust in certified, high-quality architectural hardware solutions for evolving infrastructure needs.

  • Hardwyn launched an ISI-certified Floor Spring, expanding its product portfolio.
  • Secured multiple orders from prominent institutional and government projects.
  • New product targets high-traffic commercial and institutional segments.
  • Orders reinforce demand for certified hardware in large infrastructure.
  • Strengthens company's position as a trusted architectural hardware provider.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hardwyn India Ltd | Category: New Launch

item.title: [AEQUS] Aequs clarifies share price movement is market-driven
item.description:
Aequs Ltd responded to an email from BSE Limited dated April 17, 2026, seeking clarification on its share price movement. The company affirmed its commitment to disclosing all material and price-sensitive information in compliance with Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligation and Disclosure Requirements), 2015. Aequs stated that the movement in its share price is solely attributable to market conditions and is market-driven, with no involvement from the company's management. Furthermore, Aequs confirmed that as of the current date, there is no significant or price-sensitive information that requires dissemination to investors.

  • Aequs received a BSE query regarding share price movement.
  • Company confirms all disclosures are made as per SEBI Regulation 30.
  • Share price movement is purely market-driven, not company-related.
  • No new price-sensitive information is currently available.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Aequs Ltd | Category: Clarification

item.title: [LAXMIINDIA] Laxmi India Q4 FY26: AUM up 27.34% to ₹1,626 Cr, Rating Upgraded
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Laxmi India Finance Ltd reported strong provisional operational updates for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The customer base expanded by 20.35% YoY to 42,809. Assets under management (AUM) grew significantly by 27.34% YoY to approximately ₹1,626.26 crore. Disbursements for FY26 increased by 14.32% to ₹821.43 crore. The company expanded its geographic footprint to 6 states and its branch network to 176. Cost of funds improved to 10.80%, supported by new credit lines. Additionally, Acuite Ratings & Research Limited upgraded the company's external credit rating to 'ACUITE A (Stable)' from 'ACUITE A- (Positive)', reflecting a strengthened credit profile.

  • Customer base grew 20.35% YoY to 42,809 as of March 31, 2026.
  • Assets under management (AUM) increased 27.34% YoY to ₹1,626.26 crore.
  • Disbursements rose 14.32% to ₹821.43 crore in FY26.
  • Geographic presence expanded to 6 states with 176 branches.
  • Credit rating upgraded to 'ACUITE A (Stable)' by Acuite Ratings.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Laxmi India Finance Ltd | Category: Quarterly Updates

item.title: [ESSEX] Essex Marine completes peeling capacity expansion & new Ready-to-Cook section
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Essex Marine Ltd has announced the completion of two key initiatives at its existing processing unit in Shankarpur. The company has successfully expanded its peeling capacity, enhancing its production capabilities. Additionally, a new "Ready-to-Cook" section has been established by integrating a blanching process into the existing operations. These developments are expected to improve operational efficiency and diversify the company's product offerings.

  • Essex Marine completed peeling capacity expansion at Shankarpur unit.
  • New Ready-to-Cook section added with blanching process.
  • Enhances production capabilities and product diversification.
  • Operational improvements at the existing processing unit.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Essex Marine Ltd | Category: Expansion

item.title: [HARDWYN] Hardwyn India Not a Large Corporate as per SEBI Debt Circular
item.description:
Hardwyn India Limited has confirmed that it is not identified as a "Large Corporate" as of March 31, 2026. This declaration is made in reference to the SEBI circular dated October 19, 2023 (Circular No. SEBI/HO/DDHS/DDHS-RACPOD1/P/CIR/2023/172), which outlines applicability criteria for fund raising by issuance of debt securities by large entities. The company's status means it is not subject to the specific compliance requirements for large corporates under this regulation.

  • Hardwyn India confirmed it is not a Large Corporate.
  • Status is as per SEBI's October 19, 2023 circular.
  • Circular relates to fund raising via debt securities.
  • Classification is effective as of March 31, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hardwyn India Ltd | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [SHEMAROO] Shemaroo: Bombay HC refers \u20b9133.61 Cr tax dispute to Larger Bench
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Shemaroo Entertainment Ltd. announced that the Hon'ble High Court of Judicature at Bombay, via an order dated April 17, 2026, has referred the questions of law arising in the company's writ proceedings to a Larger Bench for adjudication. The Court further directed that all interim orders, including the direction that no further action shall be taken pursuant to the impugned show cause notice/order, shall continue to remain in force until the Larger Bench decides the issues. The dispute involves an alleged inadmissible Input Tax Credit (ITC) of \u20b970.26 crores, along with interest and an equal penalty, and an additional penalty of \u20b963.35 crores under the CGST Act, 2017. Due to the continuation of interim relief, no coercive action can presently be taken against the company.

  • Bombay High Court referred Shemaroo's tax dispute to a Larger Bench for adjudication.
  • Interim orders, including a stay on coercive action, will remain in force.
  • The dispute involves alleged inadmissible ITC of \u20b970.26 crores.
  • Additional penalties of \u20b963.35 crores are also under dispute.
  • No immediate financial or operational impact due to continued interim relief.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Shemaroo Entertainment Ltd | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [BPCAP] B. P. Capital Board to consider shifting Registered Office to Delhi
item.description:
B. P. Capital Ltd. has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting for April 25, 2026, at 3:00 P.M. The key agenda items include considering and approving the relocation of the company's Registered Office from Haryana to the National Capital Territory of Delhi. This move will require a subsequent alteration of the Situation Clause within the company's Memorandum of Association. The Board will also deliberate on the notice for an Extra Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) to obtain shareholder approval for this proposed registered office shift and the necessary amendments to the Memorandum of Association.

  • Board meeting scheduled for April 25, 2026.
  • Agenda includes shifting registered office to Delhi.
  • Requires alteration of Memorandum of Association.
  • EGM notice for shareholder approval will be considered.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: B. P. Capital Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SURYACHAKRA] Suryachakra Power cancels board meeting due to auditor delay
item.description:
Suryachakra Power Corporation Ltd has announced the cancellation of its Board of Directors meeting, which was scheduled for April 28, 2026. This meeting had been previously adjourned from earlier dates in April. The company stated that the cancellation is a result of its Statutory Auditors requesting additional time to complete the Limited Review of the unaudited financial results. This development indicates a delay in the finalization and release of the company's financial performance.

  • Suryachakra Power cancelled its board meeting scheduled for April 28, 2026.
  • The meeting was previously adjourned from April 07 and April 14, 2026.
  • Cancellation is due to auditors needing more time for financial results review.
  • This implies a delay in the release of the company's unaudited financial results.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Suryachakra Power Corporation Ltd | Category: Board Meeting
